text: In this video, we present a case of fully robotic malignant retroperitoneal lymph node dissection with three-D modeling, following curative resection of colorectal cancer. Using three-D slicer, standard patient CT imaging was three-D reconstructed. The node and aorta were highlighted and superimposed on the three-D reconstruction. The TilePro input port on the DaVinci XI model was used to access the three-D modeling during surgery. Informed consent was obtained and the patient positioned in modified lithotomy. Ureteric stents were inserted.

The surgeon inserts four robotic ports and one assistant port under visual guidance. Dense adhesions of the small bowel, resulting from the patient's previous anterior resection, are encountered and divided. Subsequently, all bowel loops are reflected superiorly.

The surgeon dissects the parietal peritoneum over the right common iliac artery, identifying and preserving the left common iliac artery and both ureters. Using a monopolar energy device, the dissection continues past the aortic bifurcation. A malignant node is identified at the aortic bifurcation and sharply dissected, with live intraoperative referencing to the three-D reconstruction aiding in establishing a plane between the node and the anterior aortic wall amidst dense fibrosis.
The surgeon removes the specimen using an endo-catch bag, completing the procedure without any visible post-operative complications.
